patterns.label: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Highest-frequency email cluster this cycle (30 distinct ADO notifications across Bug/Feature/Task/Epic/Requirement). PM is passive subscriber/recipient, not the direct assignee. The classification decision — route by type, workstream, and urgency — is fully deterministic. Automation score 95 is the highest in the dataset.

patterns.label: Eval Strategy Guidance & Expert Q&A
patterns.llmRationale: PM is the sole eval expert receiving 10+ Teams pings/cycle from FDEs, PMs, and designers asking about eval depth, metric selection (groundedness, tool_call_accuracy, Compare Meaning), timing, and quality bars. BSI-91 CRITICAL bottleneck — zero delegation observed. Highest value score (94) tied with external partner enablement. Codifying this expertise is the single highest-leverage skill candidate.

patterns.label: Meeting Notes & Action Item Capture
patterns.llmRationale: PM consistently creates Loop meeting notes post-meeting with a fixed repeating structure: Agenda → Notes → Decisions/Open Questions → Action Items with owners. The workflow appears across both meeting and document signals, spanning 1:1s, team syncs, external sessions, and CAPE reviews. High automation (90) and high value (93) make this the top composite scorer.

patterns.label: External Partner & Customer Eval Sessions
patterns.llmRationale: Largest meeting time sink this cycle at 13 hours across 50+ external domains (Accenture, HCLTech, EY, KPMG groups, Avanade, Cognizant). Each session repeats the same eval enablement content — intro, methodology, metrics, tooling walkthrough. Pre-generating tailored enablement packs would dramatically reduce per-session prep overhead. Value score 94 tied for highest in dataset.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Update Report Generation
patterns.llmRationale: Team is producing weekly status artifacts in at least 4 parallel formats (done/next/blockers, bi-weekly newsletter, Cape Day slides, LT leadership summary). Signal explicitly notes teams restating identical progress in different artifacts. A single skill ingesting Loop notes and Teams summaries could emit all required formats simultaneously — classic multi-format generation gap.

patterns.label: Eval Enablement Deck & Playbook Creation
patterns.llmRationale: Multiple versions of eval intro decks, playbooks, and pitch decks created independently by different people (TFM Agent Evals, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5, eval convo decks, Evaluation.pptx). Meetings explicitly call out fragmentation and request consolidation. A reuse advisor skill could surface canonical content and flag version drift before it compounds further.

patterns.label: Skill-Detector Technical Spec Build Cycle
patterns.llmRationale: Iterative ZIP packages (skill-detector.zip, skill-detector 1.zip) containing SKILL.md + scripts + config produced in short intervals. PM is building and iterating on agent skill specs in rapid cycles. A scaffolding skill that bootstraps new specs from plain-language descriptions would dog-food the platform and reduce each spec cycle from ~60 min to ~5 min.

patterns.label: Weekly Recurring Meeting Sync Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Five distinct weekly/bi-weekly syncs observed (Evals Sync Tue, Project Crucible Mon, Manager Meeting Mon, Solution Library Mon, Golden Evals bi-weekly Thu) — all with consistent participant sets extended through August. Agenda prep and progress summaries follow a predictable structure that could be pre-populated from prior Loop notes automatically.

patterns.label: Cross-Org Navigation & VOC Routing
patterns.llmRationale: PM acts as informal routing layer between field engineers, PM team, and PG for eval-related asks and VOC feedback. People consistently ask PM to recommend DRI contacts within MCS Analytics and Eval teams. The routing logic is highly systematizable — the same contact recommendations recur and could be encoded as a context-aware contact recommender.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Distribution via Teams
patterns.llmRationale: PM regularly distributes GitHub repos, ZIPs, YAML specs, Loop components, and markdown files via Teams. A packaging and announcement template could streamline this — auto-generating the context message, linking related assets, and logging distributions for traceability.

patterns.label: Security & Compliance Alert Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Recurring Office365/Purview alerts (File Blocked, Administrator elevation, Loop workspace attention) arrive frequently with low decision complexity. High automation (89) but lower value (56) — the required action is usually minimal or administrative. Could be auto-classified and routed with minimal PM attention needed.

patterns.label: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning
patterns.llmRationale: PM runs a structured daily prep routine, updating a planning doc each morning (e.g., [Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day). The inputs — calendar events, prior day action items, flagged priorities — are consistent and M365-accessible. A skill auto-generating a structured daily briefing from M365 data could replace this manual ritual entirely.

patterns.label: Camp AIR Immersive Training Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Single multi-day event consuming 15 hours (two ~9-hour CAPE Camp AIR sessions, ~70-80% of scheduled meeting time this week). Adjacent artifacts are highly structured within the event timeline: standup notes, EOD debriefs, Loop pages, GitHub repos, hackathon summaries. A coordination skill pre-generating agendas and compiling daily summaries would reduce event overhead significantly.

patterns.label: Strategic Cross-Org Leadership Thread Engagement
patterns.llmRationale: Long-form cross-org email threads require dense content reading and considered replies (Project Crucible evals-first March 18, Declarative Agent grounding architecture, EvalCon announcements). Large cross-org audiences (CAPE, Copilot, leadership, partners). A thread synthesis and response-drafting skill would reduce deep-reading burden and maintain strategic alignment faster.

patterns.label: High-Urgency Escalation Email Drafting
patterns.llmRationale: PM switches to a distinct high-urgency escalation tone when blockers arise externally (URGENT: Degree CANNOT be Located). This writing mode has specific structural markers: direct subject line, problem statement, stakes, and requested action. A template skill for escalation drafts would ensure consistent tone, completeness, and appropriate framing every time.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Codifies PM eval expertise into an interactive advisor. Answers questions about eval depth, metric selection (groundedness, tool_call_accuracy, Compare Meaning), timing in agent lifecycle, quality bar setting, and rubric design. Directly unblocks the BSI-91 CRITICAL bottleneck where PM is the sole DRI for eval guidance across field engineers, customers, and cross-org teams — fielding 10+ Teams pings per cycle with zero delegation observed.
triggerExamples: How many eval cases do I need for a 3-intent agent?
Should I use keyword match or semantic grading for this use case?
What metrics should I track for a declarative agent?
When in the agent lifecycle should evals happen?
How do I assess groundedness for a RAG-based agent?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Unblocks BSI-91 CRITICAL bottleneck. Scales eval knowledge to 50+ external partner sessions per cycle without PM overhead. Highest value score in the dataset at 94.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Ingests a meeting transcript, Loop doc, or agenda and emits structured output: action items with owner and due-date, decisions log, open questions, follow-up email draft, and optional ADO task stubs. Replicates the PM's consistent Loop notes template (Agenda → Notes → Decisions → Action Items) automatically across all 5+ recurring meeting types observed this cycle.
triggerExamples: Extract action items from today's Camp AIR standup
Summarize the customer eval session into a Loop page
Generate post-meeting recap from CAPE Architecture Call
Create structured 1:1 notes with decisions and owners
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Saves ~1.2 hrs per cycle across 8 participants. Highest composite score (92) in the dataset. Works for all recurring meeting types.

candidateSkills.name: weekly-status-report-generator
candidateSkills.description: Aggregates weekly progress from Loop notes, Teams thread summaries, email updates, and meeting recaps. Generates all required output formats simultaneously: done/next/blockers, bi-weekly newsletter, LT leadership summary, and Cape Day slides. Directly resolves the 4-format parallel creation gap observed this cycle.
triggerExamples: Generate this week's CAPE eval status from Loop notes
Create Cape Day slides from my weekly updates
Draft the XFN Report Out from meeting summaries
Refresh the bi-weekly newsletter from all sources
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Collapses 4 parallel creation workflows into 1. Saves ~45 min per cycle across 4 roles. Estimated ~$111K/yr.

candidateSkills.name: partner-eval-enablement-pack-generator
candidateSkills.description: Generates tailored eval enablement packs for external partner and customer sessions. Includes eval intro slides adapted to partner context, methodology talking points, metric selection guide, tooling walkthrough (CLI vs in-product), and a standardized follow-up action template. Addresses the #2 time sink of the cycle: 13 hours across 50+ external domains per cycle.
triggerExamples: Prepare an eval enablement pack for our Accenture session
Generate a tailored eval intro for the KPMG customer meeting
Create an eval methodology doc for the HCL Tech engagement
Build a partner eval session follow-up template
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Reduces prep overhead for 13 hrs/cycle of external partner sessions. Scales across 50+ external domains without PM bottleneck. Value score 94.

candidateSkills.name: skill-spec-scaffold-generator
candidateSkills.description: Bootstraps new Claude skill specifications from a plain-language description of the skill's purpose and trigger conditions. Generates SKILL.md, config.json, shell scripts, and boilerplate state files. Directly dog-foods the Skilluminator platform. PM runs iterative ZIP builds every few days — this meta-skill reduces each iteration from ~60 min to ~5 min.
triggerExamples: Scaffold a new skill spec for weekly report generation
Create SKILL.md for an eval coaching advisor skill
Generate a skill ZIP for a meeting notes extractor
Bootstrap a skill-creator spec from this PRD description
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Meta-skill that accelerates all other skill development. Reduces spec iteration cycle from ~60 min to ~5 min. Rising trend. Composite score 83.
